title: Catalogue globalization
description: Decouple the product catalogue from items so that products/manufacturer-model entries, and their price observations/history, are platform-owned and shared across every project/user, instead of the current item_id-scoped schema. Introduces a new item_products join table for item-specific overrides (match terms, per-item deal-price threshold, tracking/wanted state).
notes: See docs/adr/0007-catalogue-globalization.md and docs/implementation-notes/2026-07-08-2100-catalogue-globalization-epic-100.md. Shipped 2026-07-08: 665 tests passing (654 prior + 11 new), zero regressions. Migration dry-run proven against a copy of a real historical backup (data/product_finder.db.bak.20260705T200501), not just synthetic fixtures. Real data/product_finder.db was migrated mid-session by a live watch process picking up in-progress code (see implementation notes 'Data integrity incident' section) — verified zero data loss, one minor leftover column (products.wanted, harmless, deferred cleanup). Not one of the five numbered phases in ADR-0001 — a separate, independently-sequenced data-model fix that landed before EPIC-103 as recommended. Still a hard blocking dependency before EPIC-104/EPIC-105 shipping (those phases themselves not started).
